items.text: haematite compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown with a wasm feature flag.
Filesystem-only code (DiskStore, native durable WAL) is cfg-gated out of the WASM build (CN1).
BLAKE3 uses the portable (non-SIMD) implementation on WASM and produces identical hashes to native (CN2).
WASM binary size stays under 2MB gzipped with wasm-opt size optimisation (CN3).
Shard actors run on web workers using the beamr-wasm scheduler.
IndexedDB NodeStore implements the NodeStore trait with hash as key and zstd-compressed bytes as value.
IndexedDB operations do not block the browser's main thread (CN4).
The LRU cache from the persistence cluster caches decompressed nodes in WASM linear memory.
IndexedDB NodeStore passes the same functional tests as the native DiskStore.
Node writes are idempotent: writing a hash that already exists in IndexedDB is a no-op.
OPFS WAL uses synchronous file access in web worker contexts for append-only writes.
OPFS WAL entry format is byte-identical to the native durable WAL format (CN6).
OPFS WAL includes CRC32 checksums per entry, matching the native format.
Runtime capability detection determines OPFS availability.
When OPFS is unavailable, the WAL falls back to IndexedDB-backed persistence (CN5).
Fallback is transparent to the shard actor — the WAL interface is the same regardless of backend.
Distribution sync in the WASM build operates over WebSocket data channels.
The sync protocol payload is identical to the native distribution sync — only the transport differs.
